---
title: "R Episode 12 — Data Manipulation with dplyr"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Objectives
By the end of this episode you will be able to:

- Use the core dplyr verbs: `filter`, `select`, `mutate`, `arrange`.
- Chain steps with the native pipe `|>`.
- Summarize by group with `group_by()` + `summarise()`.

**Teaching:** 40 min · **Exercises:** 30 min
:::

## The pipe

The native pipe **`|>`** (R 4.1+) passes the left value into the next function's first argument,
turning nested calls into a readable top-to-bottom pipeline. (You may also see magrittr's `%>%`
in older code — they're largely interchangeable here.)

```r
library(dplyr)

gapminder |> filter(year == 2007) |> head()
```

## The core verbs

```r
# filter(): keep rows by condition
gapminder |> filter(continent == "Europe", year == 2007)

# select(): keep/drop columns
gapminder |> select(country, year, lifeExp)

# mutate(): add/transform columns
gapminder |> mutate(gdp = pop * gdpPercap)

# arrange(): sort rows
gapminder |> arrange(desc(lifeExp))
```

## Compose a pipeline

```r
gapminder |>
  filter(continent == "Asia", year == 2007) |>
  mutate(gdp_billion = pop * gdpPercap / 1e9) |>
  select(country, lifeExp, gdp_billion) |>
  arrange(desc(gdp_billion))
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Instructor note
Read a pipeline aloud as a sentence: "take gapminder, *then* filter, *then* mutate, *then*
select…". This framing makes the pipe click. Build the pipeline one verb at a time, running after
each, so learners see it grow.
:::

## Group and summarize

```r
gapminder |>
  group_by(continent, year) |>
  summarise(
    mean_life = mean(lifeExp),
    total_pop = sum(pop),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

This single idiom replaces the loop from Episode 7 — and reads far better.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Challenge — Top of each continent
For the year 2007, find the country with the highest life expectancy on each continent.

### Solution

```r
gapminder |>
  filter(year == 2007) |>
  group_by(continent) |>
  slice_max(lifeExp, n = 1) |>
  select(continent, country, lifeExp)
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Key points
- `|>` chains operations into readable pipelines.
- `filter`/`select`/`mutate`/`arrange` cover most row/column work.
- `group_by()` + `summarise()` is the idiomatic grouped aggregation.
:::

Next: [Episode 13 — Reshaping Data with tidyr](r-13-tidyr.qmd).
